name: 08_Robot_Control_PPO_PyBullet_RL
query: Ninatafuta kutekeleza mradi unaochunguza udhibiti wa mkono wa roboti kupitia ujifunzaji wa kuimarisha katika mazingira ya simulizi ya PyBullet kwa kutumia algoriti ya PPO. Simulizi ya PyBullet inapaswa kuingizwa na mazingira yanayohusiana na roboti yanapaswa kupakiwa katika `src/env.py`. Algoriti ya PPO inapaswa kutekelezwa katika `src/train.py`. Mradi unapaswa kuandika kwa umakini nafasi ya mwisho ya roboti, kuchapisha na kuihifadhi kama `data/final_position.txt`. Trajectory ya kurudi kwa mafunzo inapaswa kuchorwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/training_returns.png`. Mfano wa mwendo wa roboti unapaswa kuonyeshwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/robot_motion.gif`. Maelezo ya kina ya usanidi wa mazingira na muundo wa zawadi yanapaswa kutolewa katika `src/env.py`. Tafadhali hakikisha kwamba masuala yoyote ya kupakia faili za URDF katika PyBullet yanashughulikiwa na kuandikwa wazi, kutoa ujumbe wa makosa wazi au kuingia kwa ajili ya utatuzi.

requirements.criteria: Maelezo ya kina ya usanidi wa mazingira na muundo wa zawadi yanatolewa katika `src/env.py`.
Nafasi ya mwisho ya roboti inachapishwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `data/final_position.txt`.
Mchoro wa kurudi kwa mafunzo kwa muda unarekodiwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/training_returns.png`.
Mfano wa mwendo wa roboti unaonyeshwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/robot_motion.gif`.

preferences.criteria: Mfumo unapaswa kushughulikia kwa ufanisi masuala yanayoweza kutokea ya kupakia faili za URDF katika PyBullet, kutoa ujumbe wa makosa wazi au kuingia kwa ajili ya utatuzi.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The content of `src/env.py` does not use PyBullet simulator at all. The file contains only a simple `Gridworld` class with basic grid navigation logic, and a list of `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` strings that merely *mention* PyBullet as text. There is no `import pybullet`, no PyBullet environment setup, no physics simulation, and no actual integration with the PyBullet library. The file is essentially a stub/placeholder that does not fulfill the requirement of actually using the PyBullet simulator.

<UNSATISFIED>: The `src/train.py` file does not implement the PPO algorithm. Instead, it contains only a minimal/stub implementation with a simple loop (`score += step`) that has no connection to PPO. The file defines `KEYWORDS = ["PPO", "ppo"]` and mentions PPO in comments/strings, but there is no actual PPO algorithm implementation — no policy network, no value function, no clipping objective, no optimizer, and no interaction with a PyBullet environment. The presence of the keyword "PPO" in a list variable does not constitute using the PPO algorithm.

judge_stats.criteria: Maelezo ya kina ya usanidi wa mazingira na muundo wa zawadi yanatolewa katika `src/env.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The content of `src/env.py` does not contain any detailed environment setup or reward structure description related to robot control with PyBullet/PPO. Instead, the file contains only a minimal stub implementation with a simple `Gridworld` class (a basic 5x5 grid with start/end points) and a list of requirement criteria strings. There is no PyBullet environment setup, no robot simulation configuration, no observation/action space definitions, no reward function, and no documentation describing the environment or reward structure for the PPO robot control task.

judge_stats.criteria: Nafasi ya mwisho ya roboti inachapishwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `data/final_position.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The file `data/final_position.txt` exists but contains only a placeholder value `('placeholder\r\n', None)` instead of actual robot position data. This indicates the file was created as a stub but was never populated with real final position coordinates from the robot simulation/training. The requirement to save the robot's actual final position has not been fulfilled.

judge_stats.criteria: Mchoro wa kurudi kwa mafunzo kwa muda unarekodiwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/training_returns.png`.

reason: <SATISFIED>: The file `results/figures/training_returns.png` exists in the project structure as shown in the workspace tree (`results\figures\training_returns.png`). Although the image content could not be read due to a media type error during evaluation, the file itself is present in the expected location, confirming it was generated and saved by the training process.

judge_stats.criteria: Mfano wa mwendo wa roboti unaonyeshwa na kuhifadhiwa kama `results/figures/robot_motion.gif`.

reason:
<SATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/robot_motion.gif` exists in the project structure as confirmed by the workspace tree. Although the trajectory evidence does not record the creation steps, and the file reading attempt failed due to a media type mismatch error (the system tried to read it as JPEG but it is a GIF), the file itself is present at the correct path `results/figures/robot_motion.gif`. The existence of the file satisfies the requirement that the robot motion visualization is saved as `results/figures/robot_motion.gif`.
